title: Professional Training & Capacity Building
text:

Workshops on point pattern analysis, spatial regression, and interpolation

Species distribution modeling using modern computational tools

Advanced raster analysis and automated mapping

Building reproducible geospatial scripts and applications

title: Geospatial Consulting
text:
We provide tailored geospatial strategies designed to address both site‑specific and regional environmental challenges. Our work integrates spatial intelligence, environmental science, and advanced analytics to support informed decision‑making.



Assessing spatial patterns and environmental change over time

Identifying ecological trends and landscape‑level drivers

Supporting conservation, restoration, and land‑use planning





Mapping and quantifying environmental hazards

Evaluating exposure, vulnerability, and operational risks

Integrating geospatial intelligence into risk‑mitigation strategies

section: We deliver data-driven intelligence where it matters most.
text: We deliver data-driven intelligence where it matters most.

Three researchers, one landscape-sized problem
Prime Geospatial began as a collaborative effort among three long-time friends and doctoral researchers. Today, Prime Geospatial serves as the vital link between raw computing and actionable ground truth.
We handle the heavy lifting — accessing, processing, maintaining, and analyzing complex datasets — while uniquely providing the expert interpretation and essential validation framework that automated geospatial models lack.
We turn raw, intimidating data into clear, verified, and deeply understood spatial insights that drive smarter, faster, and more cost-effective decision-making.

Beyond the map
We don’t believe in siloed data. By integrating remote sensing with deep social-ecological theory, we provide more than just maps, we provide context. Our team lives at the intersection of climate science, ecology, and human geography, ensuring that every spatial insight is grounded in rigorous, field-validated reality, not just routine GIS processing.
Our workflows are built on a foundation of open, reproducible science and peer-reviewed methodology. Through this transdisciplinary approach, we deliver the high-fidelity diagnostics our partners need to plan, protect, and prove their impact with total confidence.
While our methodology is global in its scientific rigor, our focus is grounded in the complex social-ecological landscapes of East Africa. We believe that deep, place-based knowledge is the essential final layer of geospatial analysis, ensuring our diagnostics are not just accurate, but locally transformative.

section: Bridging the Gap Between Data and Discovery
text:
Bridging the Gap Between Data and Discovery
Explore Our Services

section: Turning landscape data into trusted intelligence for sustainable futures.
text: Turning landscape data into trusted intelligence for sustainable futures.

ArdhiLytics
ArdhiLytics is a science-driven spatial analytics and environmental intelligence firm.
We transform complex landscape data into actionable evidence that helps governments, conservation organizations, investors, project developers, and communities make confident decisions. By integrating satellite observation, advanced spatial modeling, field-based data, and social-ecological insights, we deliver audit-ready intelligence that connects environmental outcomes with real-world impact.
Built on a foundation of scientific rigor, transparency, and local relevance, we work at the intersection of conservation, climate, and sustainable development. Our expertise spans carbon measurement and verification, land-use change modeling, landscape ecology, resilience assessment, and evidence-based planning. We bridge spatial and social science, combining ecological data, community knowledge, and governance dynamics to provide a complete understanding of how landscapes function and change over time.
From project design and baseline assessments to monitoring, verification, and impact evaluation, we help our partners move beyond assumptions and make decisions backed by credible, measurable evidence.

section: Five layers of analysis — one landscape.
text:
Five layers of analysis — one landscape.
A quick look at the stack. Each layer is available on its own, or combined into a full landscape assessment.

Social-ecological integration

section: Have a landscape that needs a closer look?
text:
Have a landscape that needs a closer look?
Tell us what you’re trying to prove, protect, or plan for. Let us discuss which layers apply.

We bridge the gap between ecological claims and verifiable reality. Using satellite data, advanced modeling, and local social-ecological insights, we provide donors, developers, investors, land managers and governments across the region, with the definitive, audit-ready proof needed to secure and sustain your land-use investments.


View services Start a project






Services


Each service reads like a layer in a GIS project — independently useful, and stronger together. Pick one, or let us build the full stack for a project.






LAYER 01




High-integrity, audit-ready carbon intelligence that you can bank on.

We provide independent, satellite-grounded measurement-reporting-verification (MRV) pipelines designed to meet the strictest registry standards. By integrating precise biomass estimation, automated land-cover change detection, and longitudinal monitoring, we create a transparent data trail that transforms project performance into reliable, investable assets.
Whether you are developing a new project or vetting a portfolio, our rigorous verification ensures your credits withstand third-party scrutiny and satisfy the most demanding due diligence.


Who it’s for
Project developers Conservancies Impact investors

02°10’S 38°04’E
Illustrative anchor: Tsavo–Kasigau region











LAYER 02




Co-designing the future of your landscape.

Static maps don’t capture the pulse of a changing region. We provide dynamic, community-guided scenario modeling that integrates local values and future needs into every projection. From mitigating agricultural sprawl to managing urban growth, we help you build land-use strategies that align conservation goals with sustainable community development.
Our approaches are precision-engineered for spatial planning, regulatory compliance, and high-impact donor reporting.


Who it’s for
County governments Land-use planners Conservation NGOs

00°49’S 35°42’E
Illustrative anchor: Mau Forest Complex







Aerial view of forest edge and farmland near the Mau Forest Complex








LAYER 03




Bridging the gap between ecological integrity and human prosperity.

We specialize in mapping and modeling the complexities of fragmented landscapes to create actionable conservation strategies. Our work goes beyond static mapping, utilizing cutting-edge simulation to show where conservation investments and community-led agreements will actually hold, not just where a map says they should.
Our Core Focus Areas:

Connectivity & corridor design
Proactive human-wildlife conflict modeling
Integrated well-being assessments
Effectiveness & impact analysis



Who it’s for
Wildlife conservancies Park authorities Conservation NGOs

00°02’N 37°33’E
Illustrative anchor: Northern Rangelands







Wildlife corridor landscape in Kenya’s Northern Rangelands








LAYER 04




Where land, climate, and community governance converge.

Effective conservation requires more than satellite imagery; it requires understanding the governance systems that sustain the land. We pair spatial data with social science to model resilience, assess land governance, and evaluate community-based natural resource management.
By projecting the impacts of interventions before they occur and auditing their performance after implementation, we provide the integrated intelligence necessary to turn conservation goals into lasting, on-the-ground resilience.
Our specialized assessments include:

Landscape & regenerative planning
Restoration & intervention analysis
Climate adaptation



Who it’s for
Research consortia Bilateral donors Conservancy associations

03°04’S 37°21’E
Illustrative anchor: Kilimanjaro socio-ecological zone







Community grazing land near Kilimanjaro’s socio-ecological zone








LAYER 05




Translating complex spatial data into actionable, team-led expertise.

We provide the analytical heavy lifting, from advanced statistical modeling to custom R and Python workflows, so your team can move faster and smarter. More than just a service provider, we build internal capacity through hands-on workshops and deep-dive consulting, empowering your staff to manage complex environmental challenges long after our engagement ends.


Who it’s for
Conservation NGOs and non-Profits Government environmental agencies Carbon and biodiversity project developers

02°17’N 37°59’E
Illustrative anchor: Advanced GIS for conservation practitioners







Dry rangeland landscape near Marsabit, Northern Kenya











Our approach


Every service we provide is anchored in three non-negotiable technical pillars:





A




We go beyond remote sensing pixels by integrating high-fidelity ground truth data including field plots, transects, and bioacoustic monitoring to calibrate our models. This multi-source data fusion ensures our spatial projections reflect actual on-the-ground ecological conditions.



B




We integrate social science with spatial analysis to capture the human dimensions of land management. By embedding local governance frameworks and communal knowledge directly into our modeling engines (e.g., Agent-Based Models), we ensure our assessments are socially sustainable and operationally viable.



C




Our workflows are built for auditability. We utilize open-science standards to ensure every output meets the strict requirements of carbon registries and institutional auditors. By prioritizing documentation and transparency from the start, we eliminate the need for costly rework during final verification.







Sectors




Carbon & Conservation Finance Government & County Planning Multilaterals & Bilateral Donors Research Institutions Agriculture & Insurance






Tell us what you’re trying to prove, protect, or plan for. We’ll tell you which layers apply.
